verses.commentary: Twelve disciples — corresponding to the twelve tribes, signaling restoration of Israel. Jesus delegates his own authority (exousia, ἐξουσία) over demons and disease. The mission outsources, but the power source is unchanged.
Twelve named — list begins with Simon Peter. 'First' (protos, πρῶτος) marks rank, not chronology. Andrew (his brother), James and John (Zebedee's sons). The Galilean fishermen lead.
Philip and Bartholomew, Thomas and Matthew (Matthew the tax collector — the author signs his shameful résumé). James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus. Diverse social profiles.

Simon the Zealot (Jewish anti-Roman faction) and Matthew the tax collector (Roman collaborator) share the same circle. Judas Iscariot — name listed with the betrayal already attached. The community holds enemies in tension.
First sending limited: not to Gentiles, not to Samaritans. Israel must hear first (compare Romans 1:16). This phase precedes the post-resurrection Great Commission (28:19).
'Lost sheep of the house of Israel' — Ezekiel 34's diagnosis. The Twelve become subordinate shepherds before the chief Shepherd's return at the end (10:23).
The message is identical to John's and Jesus' own: 'The kingdom of heaven has come near.' The Twelve inherit not a new gospel but the same one.
Same activities Jesus performs: heal, raise, cleanse, drive out demons. 'Freely you received, freely give' — the supply is grace, the principle is non-commercial. Spiritual gifts cannot be monetized.
No money in belts — no working capital. The mission lives on God's provision through hospitality, not on accumulated funds.
No bag, no extra tunic, no sandals (or staff). Total mobility, total dependence. 'The worker deserves his keep' — provision comes through reception, not preparation.
Find a 'worthy' (axios, ἄξιος) household — receptive home — and stay. No comparison shopping for better accommodations. Stability of base over hospitality-hunting.
'Give it your greeting' (aspasasthe, ἀσπάσασθε) — shalom-blessing. The disciple's peace is not formula but actual currency of the kingdom.
Peace rests if worthy; returns if not. Blessing is reactive to reception — it does not coerce. The kingdom's peace can be welcomed or refused.
Shake the dust off the feet — a Jewish gesture against Gentile defilement. Now reversed against unreceptive Israelite towns: they have placed themselves in the position of Gentiles.
Sodom and Gomorrah more bearable on judgment day — Israelite towns face harsher judgment because they had more revelation. Knowledge increases responsibility.
Sheep among wolves — vulnerability is permanent. Shrewd (phronimoi, φρόνιμοι, snake-wise) and innocent (akeraioi, ἀκέραιοι, dove-pure). Wisdom and integrity together — neither alone suffices.
Beware of men — handed over to councils (synedria, συνέδρια) and flogged in synagogues. The institutional persecution will come from inside Israel before Rome.
Before governors and kings — gospel reaches power structures through prosecution. 'A testimony to them and to the Gentiles' — even hostile courts become evangelistic venues.
Do not be anxious about defense speech (merimnesete, μεριμνήσητε — verbal anxiety again, compare 6:25). The words will be given.
Not you speaking but the Spirit of your Father. Trinitarian theology embedded: Father gives words through the Spirit on behalf of the Son. Christology in action.
Family betrayal — Micah 7:6 imagery. The gospel divides households. Loyalty to Jesus cuts horizontally across kin lines, and the cut bleeds.
Hated by all on account of Jesus' name. 'The one who endures (hypomeinas, ὑπομείνας) to the end will be saved.' Endurance, not flash, marks the genuine disciple.

Flee to the next town under persecution — mobility, not martyrdom-seeking. 'You will not finish the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes' — debated reference, possibly to vindication at Jerusalem's fall or beyond.
Disciple not above teacher, servant not above master. The hierarchy of Jesus shapes the disciple's expectations: do not expect better treatment than the rabbi.
If they call the master Beelzebul (literally 'lord of the flies' — Philistine deity), how much more the household? Identification with Jesus brings his slanders.
Do not fear — concealed will be revealed. Final disclosure is guaranteed. The disciples' present obscurity is temporary.
Whispered teaching to be proclaimed from rooftops. The intimate becomes the universal. No content held back for elites.
Fear God who can destroy both soul and body in hell (gehenna, γέεννα), not those who can only kill the body. Recalibrate the fear hierarchy. The greatest threat is misidentified.
Two sparrows for a penny (assarion, ἀσσάριον — smallest copper coin). Not one falls apart from the Father. Even cheap creatures are inside divine awareness.
Hairs of your head numbered (erithmemenai, ἠρίθμημέναι). Hyperbolic specificity. Providence is granular, not just structural.
You are worth more than many sparrows. Fear dissolves in the awareness of God's valuation. Stop fearing.
Public confession of Jesus before others reciprocated by Jesus' confession of you before the Father. Public risk, public reward.
Denial inverts the same logic. Whoever denies Jesus before others — denied before the Father. The earthly transaction has an eschatological correlate.
Not peace but a sword. The pacifist Messiah produces social conflict. The peace of Christ in the world causes precisely this disturbance.
Micah 7:6 quoted. Father-son, mother-daughter, mother-in-law-daughter-in-law: the close relationships fracture along the Jesus-line.
Enemies will be members of one's own household. The gospel cuts where family loyalty alone cannot reach. Hard truth said hard.
Loving father/mother/son/daughter more than Jesus = unworthy of him. The Messiah claims a place above first-order kin loyalty. Functional supremacy.
Cross-bearing — first cross-saying in Matthew. The cross is the Roman execution-tool; carrying it meant walking to one's own death. Discipleship is condemned-prisoner identity.
Saving life loses it; losing life for Jesus finds it. The paradox is operational, not rhetorical. Self-preservation is self-destruction.
Reception chain: receiving the disciple = receiving Jesus = receiving the Father. Hospitality to the messenger is hospitality to the sender.
Receive a prophet because he is a prophet — gain the prophet's reward. Hospitality with discernment. Recognizing the office brings the blessing.
Cup of cold water to a 'little one' (mikros, μικρός — a disciple of lesser status). Even smallest gestures count, when given in the name. Reward not lost.
